perturbation.original_text: 4.Return or Destruction of Confidential Information. Upon written demand by the Disclosing Party , the Receiving Party shall: (i) cease using the Confidential Information; (ii) promptly return the Confidential Information and all copies, notes or extracts thereof to the Disclosing Party within seven (7)da ys of receipt of demand; and/or (iii) upon request of the Disclosing Party, destroy all such Confidential Information, including copies thereof, and shall furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.Return or Destruction of Confidential Information. Upon written demand by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall: (i) cease using the Confidential Information; and/or (iii) upon request of the Disclosing Party, destroy all such Confidential Information, including copies thereof, and shall furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the obligation to return the Confidential Information within seven (7) days, Section 4 creates a contradiction because the only remaining obligations are to cease using it and destroy it. Section 6 which discusses 'ownership' becomes contradictory, as destruction is now the default, undermining the Disclosing Party's continued ownership of information they may prefer to have back rather than destroyed. The Disclosing Party can no longer guarantee receiving back that information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.Ownership and Disclaimer. Each party shall retain all right, title and interest to such party’s Confidential Information disclosed to the other party. Subject only to the Receiving Party’s limited use of the Confidential I nformation for the purpose set forth above, the Receiving Party acknowledges a nd ag rees that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or implying any rights, license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement whether under any trademark, patent or copyright, or application of same which are now or thereafter may be obtained by such party. The Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party’s intellectual property orother rights in or to the Confidential Information. AL L CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED “AS IS” WITHOUT ANY R EPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. I N NO EVENT SHALL THE DISCLOSING PARTY BE LIABLE FOR THE ACCURACY OR COMPLETENESS OF ANY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Disclosing Party to demand return of Confidential Information within 7 days. The changed text removes this obligation, leaving only the option to cease using or destroy the information. This contradicts Section 6, which states that the Disclosing Party retains all right, title, and interest to the Confidential Information. By removing the return option, the Disclosing Party may be forced to have their information destroyed even if they prefer to retain ownership, thus conflicting with their ownership rights as outlined in Section 6.

perturbation.original_text: 5.Injunctive Relief. The provisions of this Agreement are necessary for the protection of the business of each party and are considered by the other party to be reasonable for such purpose. Each party agrees that any breach of this Agreement may cause the other party substantial and irreparable damages and, therefore, in the event of any such breach, in addition to other remedies that may be available, each party shall have the right to seek specific performance and other injunctive and equitable relief against the other.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.Injunctive Relief. The provisions of this Agreement are necessary for the protection of the business of each party and are considered by the other party to be reasonable for such purpose. Each party agrees that any breach of this Agreement may cause the other party substantial and irreparable damages and, therefore, in the event of any such breach, in addition to other remedies that may be available, each party shall have the right to seek specific performance and other injunctive and equitable relief.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'against the other', Section 5 creates an in-text contradiction. While it states the parties can seek injunctive relief, it no longer explicitly states that such relief can be sought against the *other* party. This undermines the purpose of the section, which is to provide a legal recourse against the breaching party. Section 9.5 (No Waiver) now becomes contradictory as the ability to enforce injunctive relief is weakened, making any perceived 'waiver' of rights more impactful due to the reduced enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.5 No Waiver . No waiver b y either party of any breach or default of any of the covenants or agreements herein contained shall be deemed a waiver as to any subsequent or similar breach or default. No right or remedy herein conferred upon either party is exclusive of any other right or remedy herein or by law or in equity provided or permitted.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'against the other' in Section 5 weakens the enforceability of injunctive relief. This directly contradicts Section 9.5 (No Waiver), as the diminished ability to enforce injunctive relief makes any potential 'waiver' of rights more significant due to the reduced legal recourse. The original text clearly allowed for injunctive relief against the other party, while the changed text introduces ambiguity, leading to a conflict with Section 9.5 regarding the implications of waiving rights.

perturbation.original_text: 8.No Obligation to Do Business . Each party acknowledges and agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall impose upon either party any obligation to consummate a transaction. Subject to the terms andconditions of this Agreement, discussions and communications between the parties hereto shall not impair the right of either party to develop, make, use, procure and/or market any products or services (now or in the future) which may be competitive with those offered by the other party, provided none of the foregoing activities violate the term s of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.No Obligation to Do Business . Each party acknowledges and agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall impose upon either party any obligation to consummate a transaction. Discussions and communications between the parties hereto shall not impair the right of either party to develop, make, use, procure and/or market any products or services (now or in the future) which may be competitive with those offered by the other party, provided none of the foregoing activities violate the term s of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement', Section 8 now implies that the right to develop competing products is absolute, even if it violates the confidentiality provisions of the NDA. This directly contradicts the purpose of the NDA, especially Section 2 (Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information), which restricts the use of confidential information. Section 2 and 8 are now in direct contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. The party receiving Confidential Information (the “Receiving Party”) shall no t disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the party disclosing such confidential information (the “Disclosing Party”) and shallrestrict dissemination of Confidential Information within its own organization to those employees who have a need to have access to the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party agrees to protectConfidential Information by using the same degree of care as it would use to protect its own information of like importance, but in no case less than reasonable care. Any use of Confidential Information shall be solely for the purpose authorized above. Each party shall take appropriate measures, and in no event less than reasonable measures, by instruction and agreement prior to disclo sure to such employees to assure against unauthorized use or disclosure. The Receiving Party agrees not to reverse engineer,disassemble, or otherwise restructure and/or copy or in any way recreate in whole or in part a ny C onfidential Information of the D isclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the right to develop competing products was 'Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement'. The changed text omits this phrase, thereby creating a contradiction with Section 2 (Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information). Now Section 8 implies that the right to develop competing products is absolute, even if it violates the confidentiality provisions. Section 2 restricts the use of confidential information. This is a direct conflict as the changed Section 8 allows actions that Section 2 prohibits.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 Notice . Any notice, approval, request, authorization, direction or other communication required or permitted under this Agreement shall be given in writing and shall be deemed to have been delivered: (i) one (1) business day after deposit with a commercial overnight courier with written verification of receipt; or (ii) five (5) business days after the mailing date if sent by U.S. mail, return receipt requested, postage and charges prepaid. Notice shall be given to each party at the addresses set forth above.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 Notice . Any notice, approval, request, authorization, direction or other communication required or permitted under this Agreement shall be given in writing and shall be deemed to have been delivered: (i) one (1) business day after deposit with a commercial overnight courier with written verification of receipt. Notice shall be given to each party at the addresses set forth above.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (ii), Section 9.3 eliminates U.S. mail as an acceptable method for delivering notices. This creates a contradiction because it could potentially conflict with state laws (referenced implicitly in 9.2 via choice of law) that recognize certified mail as a valid form of legal notice. The altered clause forces reliance solely on courier services. Now section 4, regarding Returning or Destruction of Confidential information becomes contraditory, as it's unclear the legitimacy of sending the demand via US Mail.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.Return or Destruction of Confidential Information. Upon written demand by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall: (i) cease using the Confidential Information; and/or (iii) upon request of the Disclosing Party, destroy all such Confidential Information, including copies thereof, and shall furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 9.3 removes U.S. mail as an acceptable method for delivering notices. This directly contradicts Section 4, which refers to "written demand." The original text allows for this demand to be sent via U.S. mail, return receipt requested. The changed text makes the legitimacy of sending the demand via US Mail unclear, creating a strong contradiction that impacts the enforceability of the demand in Section 4.

perturbation.original_text: 9.4 Assignment and Delegation . This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party, without the prior written consent of the other party. Any purported or attempted assignment hereof without such written consent shall be void and of no force or effect. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of any successors.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.4 Assignment and Delegation . This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party, without the prior written consent of the other party. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of any successors.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'Any purported or attempted assignment hereof without such written consent shall be void and of no force or effect.' from Section 9.4, the agreement now states that assignment requires consent, but does not explicitly state the consequences of assigning the contract without consent. This introduces ambiguity and an internal contradiction, making Section 9.6 (Entire Agreement) contradictory, because the lack of clarity on unapproved assignment creates uncertainty in the complete terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.6 Entire Agreement. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto pertaining to the subject matter hereof, and is the final, complete and exclusive expression of t he terms and conditions thereof. All prior or contemporaneous agreements, representations, negotiations and understandings of the parties hereto, oral or written, express or implied, are hereby superseded and merged herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that any assignment without written consent is void. The changed text removes this consequence, creating ambiguity about the effect of an unapproved assignment. This directly contradicts Section 9.6, which states that the agreement is the "entire agreement" and a "complete and exclusive expression" of the terms, because the consequences of unapproved assignment are no longer definitively addressed. The omission weakens the enforceability of the contract and introduces an internal contradiction.
